steps.title: Identify the sentence type
steps.content: The sentence describes a historical fact about when a time period came to be known as the Reconstruction era—it's a statement, not a question.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
The sentence is a factual statement about history, not a question. Also, "which" already serves as the subject of the relative clause — adding "it" creates a double subject.
  • Choice A — "come to be known as the Reconstruction era?": "...1865 and 1877, which would come to be known as the Reconstruction era?" — question mark is wrong for a declarative statement. ✗
  • Choice B — "come to be known as the Reconstruction era.": "...1865 and 1877, which would come to be known as the Reconstruction era." — "which" is the subject, period closes the statement correctly. ✓
  • Choice C — "it come to be known as the Reconstruction era.": "...1865 and 1877, which would it come to be known as the Reconstruction era." — "which" and "it" are both subjects for the same clause; double subject error. ✗
  • Choice D — "it come to be known as the Reconstruction era?": Same double subject as C, plus wrong question mark. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
